page: Microsoft Azure E48s_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ESv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes a cache hierarchy of 48 KB L1d, 1 MB L2, and 48 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Performance benchmarks place the E48s_v5 in the average to strong tiers depending on the workload. It demonstrates strong capabilities in Passmark database operations and Passmark memory scores, alongside strong Geekbench image processing and Passmark CPU encryption results. Multi-core performance is solid, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 46608.74 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 15644.0. Large block memory bandwidth reaches 160.19 GB/sec for read operations and 67.22 GB/sec for write operations.

A primary tradeoff of this instance is the complete absence of local storage, requiring reliance on external Premium Storage options. Additionally, without GPU acceleration, the server is poorly suited for large-scale AI tasks, as evidenced by a poor text generation speed of 2.4 tokens/sec on a 70B LLM model. However, its high memory density and strong database operation benchmarks make it highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads. It is best suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, enterprise applications, and data processing tasks that fit within its 384 GB memory footprint.
description: Microsoft Azure E48s_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ESv5 family, powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and GPU resources. Running on Microsoft Hyper-V, the instance delivers strong performance in database operations, memory benchmarks, and encryption tasks, while multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier. Tradeoffs include a reliance on external Premium Storage and poor performance for large-scale LLM text generation. This server is highly suited for memory-intensive enterprise applications, relational databases, and in-memory caching.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48s_v5 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong database and memory performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48s_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 384 GB RAM, designed for databases and memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high memory density and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for demanding database and enterprise workloads.

High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core
Strong performance in database operations and memory benchmarks
No local storage, requiring external Premium Storage
Best suited for relational databases, caching, and memory-heavy enterprise apps
